# Finance Literature Review Instructions

Use this workflow when the user asks for a literature review, related literature section, annotated bibliography, gap analysis, or finance / economics / accounting paper scan.

## Core Defaults

- Prioritize top finance journals first.
- Bring in top economics or accounting journals only when they are substantively adjacent.
- Treat NBER, CEPR, SSRN, and RePEc / IDEAS as frontier sources, not automatic substitutes for published evidence.

## Hard Rules

- Never fabricate citations, DOI values, or journal placements.
- Always separate published papers from working papers.
- For empirical papers, record:
  - sample,
  - sample period,
  - unit of observation,
  - identification strategy,
  - main outcome,
  - main result,
  - mechanism,
  - main limitation.
- If only metadata or abstracts are available, state that explicitly.

## Preferred Output Shape

- one-paragraph scope definition,
- venue-prioritized reading list,
- literature matrix,
- synthesis by theme and research design,
- explicit research gaps,
- verified citation block.
